commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers skill export page size from 1000 to 250, rejects HTTP export requests with `limit > 250`, and adds handler/internal regression tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows current main allows/defaults export pages up to 1000 and the PR body reports production failures at 500, but no diagnostic output is attached.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer judgment on the public API compatibility change and contributor-provided real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated repair beyond normal review.

target.title: fix: cap skill export page size
target.body:
## Summary

- cap skill export pages at 250 items in both the HTTP handler and internal date-range query
- default export requests to the proven 250 item page size
- reject `limit > 250` with `400` instead of allowing oversized ZIP generation attempts
- add regression coverage for default limit, oversized limit rejection, and internal query cap

## Context

Production verification showed `limit=250` succeeds across multiple cursor pages over a 30-day range, while `limit=500` consistently returns a server error after roughly 12 seconds. This keeps mirror sync reliable through cursor pagination rather than oversized single-request ZIP generation.

## Verification

- `bun run test convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/skills.exportList.test.ts`
- `bun run format:check -- convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/skills.ts convex/skills.exportList.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run lint`
- `git diff --check`
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 /Users/patrickerichsen/Git/openclaw/openclaw/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
